references.key: the fraction 10 4 0 01 of parameters is a robust choice
references.raw: Scale-invariant default: Set k as a fraction of the total parameter count: k = ⌊α · n⌋ with α ∈ [10−5 , 10−3 ]. The fraction α = 10−4 (0.01% of parameters) is a robust choice. For example, a model with n = 106 parameters
references.title: The fraction α = 10−4 (0.01% of parameters) is a robust choice

references.key: the optimal k typically lies in the range wher
references.raw: Validation-based tuning: Perform a grid search, e.g., k ∈ {n/100000, n/10000, n/1000} (i.e., α ∈ {10−5 , 10−4 , 10−3 }), monitoring the generalization gap on a held-out set. The optimal k typically lies in the range wher
references.title: The optimal k typically lies in the range wher

references.title: If Bk < 0.01 persistently, k is too small (the optimizer is not probing enough of the landscape)
